question: How does the NuScale operating pressure in Table 1.2-1 compare to the LTOP setpoints in Table 5.2-10?

nuscale_ch01_0557.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.8 RG 8.10
nuscale_ch01_0557.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, not applicable, or partially/conforms, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. The document demonstrates a structured approach to regulatory compliance, noting specific areas of full, partial, or non-applicability.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, clarifies the use of representative information for context, and notes how security-related details are handled. The section also references figures illustrating the plant layout and system boundaries.

nuscale_ch01_0228.title: DSRS 7.2.8, Rev 0: Auxiliary FeaturesAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0228.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for auxiliary features and related systems. It notes that for DSRS 7.2.8 (Auxiliary Features), there are no specific acceptance criteria, but overall, the NuScale design conforms to applicable standards and guidance across multiple DSRS sections, including setpoints, control of access, system interactions, and display/monitoring requirements. Where partial conformance or additional references are noted, further details are provided in referenced tables.

nuscale_ch01_0227.title: DSRS 7.2.7, Rev 0: Setpoints II.3 Generic Letter (GL) 91-04
nuscale_ch01_0227.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for nuclear safety-related instrumentation setpoints and control systems. It confirms that NuScale’s setpoint methodology aligns with NRC guidance, including Generic Letter 91-04, and relevant industry standards such as ISA-67.04.01-2006 and IEEE Std 7-4.3.2-2003. The document also notes areas of full or partial compliance with additional regulatory guides for auxiliary features, control access, system interactions, and monitoring requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design with passive safety systems that do not rely on electrical power. The NPM is partially immersed in a reactor pool, has dedicated emergency cooling and decay heat removal systems, and allows for flexible power maneuvering through control rods and boron concentration adjustments. The Nuclear Steam Supply System (NSSS) integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within the reactor pressure vessel, further enhancing safety and efficiency.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0160.title: DSRS 3.11, Rev. 0: Environmental Qualification of Mechanical and Electrical EquipmentII.25 Design and Procurement Specifications
nuscale_ch01_0160.summary: This section outlines NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to the environmental qualification of mechanical and electrical equipment, as well as design and procurement specifications. It notes that certain requirements, such as DSRS 3.11 II.25, are not applicable to NuScale and are instead items for the COL applicant, while other standards (e.g., SRP 3.12 and 3.13) are confirmed as being met. Additionally, guidance specific to BWR plants is marked as not applicable to NuScale’s design.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features. It details whether each Regulatory Guide (RG) is applicable, not applicable, conforms, or partially conforms, with references to relevant sections for further information. The table provides a structured overview of compliance status for each guide, supporting regulatory review and transparency.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0706.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.B Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0706.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets the regulatory criteria outlined in DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5 for events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, closure of the main steam isolation valve, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), specifically regarding fuel cladding integrity. It notes that NuScale does not have a steam pressure regulator and that the design conforms to requirements for incidents of moderate frequency, instrument setpoints, single failures, and performance of nonsafety-related systems, with no exceptions or additional comments.

nuscale_ch01_0709.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.E Most Limiting Plant System Single Failure
nuscale_ch01_0709.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets regulatory criteria for events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), even though NuScale does not have a steam pressure regulator. For each scenario, the design is shown to conform to requirements for fuel cladding integrity, incidents of moderate frequency, instrument setpoints, most limiting single failures, and performance of nonsafety-related systems, with no exceptions or additional comments noted.

nuscale_ch01_0722.title: DSRS 15.2.7, Rev 0: Loss of Normal Feedwater FlowII.1 Fuel and System Pressure Parameters met
nuscale_ch01_0722.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various design basis events, including loss of normal feedwater flow and loss of nonemergency AC power. For each event, the document confirms compliance with system pressure, fuel integrity, and analytical modeling requirements, noting that NuScale categorizes events as anticipated operational occurrences (AOO) and infrequent events (IE), and applies conservative analytical methods as required.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and safety systems, noting that NuScale uses significantly smaller modules, fewer components, and simplified systems compared to conventional PWRs. The provided tables summarize these distinctions for reference and comparison.

nuscale_ch01_0715.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.3.D Values of Parameters Used in Analytical Model - Instrumentation Setpoints for Mitigating System Actuation
nuscale_ch01_0715.summary: This section confirms that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report meets the regulatory criteria outlined in DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5 for various transient events, including loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed). Specifically, it states that the analytical model's parameters—such as instrumentation setpoints for mitigating system actuation—conform to the requirements, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0705.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.A Reactor Coolant System and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0705.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various transient events affecting the reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, such as loss of external load, turbine trip, and main steam isolation valve closure. It notes that NuScale generally conforms to the requirements, except for partial conformance regarding the steam pressure regulator, as the NuScale design does not include this component.

nuscale_ch01_0769.title: SRP 15.6.1, Rev 2: Inadvertent Opening of a PWR Pressurizer Relief Valve or a BWR Pressure Relief ValveAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0769.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 15.6.1, which addresses the inadvertent opening of pressurizer or pressure relief valves, because it does not use power-operated relief valves (PORVs) that are susceptible to inadvertent operation. Instead, NuScale uses spring-loaded ASME code safety relief valves, and any mechanical failure is bounded by analyses of inadvertent ECCS valve actuation. Thus, the guidance is only partially applicable to NuScale’s design.
